input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text: [0-2] Centered white text "- Nintendo -" and "Presents" displays on a black background, quickly fading until faint. [2-4] A title screen appears with "SUPER MARIO WORLD" in multi-colored bubbly letters above a spotted white egg, framed by tan stone blocks and "1990-1991 Nintendo" text. [4-6] The background transitions to an outdoor scene with blue sky and mountains. Mario stands left while an orange dinosaur appears right. A menu lists "MARIO A", "MARIO B", "MARIO C", and "ERASE DATA" with a red cursor. [6-8] The menu changes to "1 PLAYER GAME" and "2 PLAYER GAME". The cursor points to the top option before the screen rapidly fades into darkness.

recent_thinks: [8-10] The dark screen fades into a bright overhead map labeled "YOSHI'S HOUSE" at the top. A small figure in red overalls appears on a path surrounded by green hills and blue water. The figure is then shown riding a white dinosaur-like creature, while a counter reading "x3" appears near the top left corner next to a small icon.

[10-12] The map header text updates to "YOSHI'S ISLAND 1" while the small figure in red overalls stands alone on the path, separated from the white dinosaur. Finally, the view cuts to a black background featuring the centered yellow text "MARIO START !".

[12-14] The map cuts to black with yellow text 'MARIO START !'. The view shifts to a side-scrolling scene with a character in red overalls on grass. A display appears at the top showing lives, score, time, and coins. On the right, an orange enemy sits on a dirt slope before large blue hills.

[14-16] The character in red overalls jumps into the air while the timer counts down from 300. The orange enemy moves from the dirt slope to the ground, appearing flattened. A gold coin appears on the far right edge as the character travels rightward across the grassy terrain.

[16-18] The character in red overalls lands on the grass and runs right. A gold coin floating near a blue pillar disappears, updating the top-right display to show one coin and 1000 points. The timer decreases to 294. The flattened orange enemy is no longer visible as the character moves forward across the green terrain.

This hand transitions briefly to an extended index pose before returning to the snapping configuration, holding it steady. Finally, the hand rapidly executes the snap, blurring significantly, while the person in the helmet remains stationary throughout these specific movements.

From 56 to 58 seconds, the individual stabilizes both hands at shoulder height, flanking the helmet symmetrically against a yellow background. Both hands maintain a specific snapping configuration where the thumbs and middle fingers are pressed together, while the index fingers point directly upward. This steady, dual-handed pose marks the final observed gesture sequence, distinct from the previous single-hand movements or transitional drops described earlier in the clip.

From 58 to 60 seconds, the individual maintains the static, symmetrical snapping pose with index fingers pointing up. At 60 seconds, they break this stillness, curling fingers inward and moving both hands downward with visible motion blur to execute a snap. Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

Coyle and Herbert B. Tanowitz. [4-6] A bright yellow highlight appears over the phrase "the most common cause of acquired epilepsy worldwide" in the text. [6-12] The scene remains static, showing the highlighted article at the bottom with the nutrition labels and logo unchanged in the background.

[20-24] A new document titled "Neurocysticercosis: Fireflies in the Central Nervous System" by Petra N. Davidson replaces the previous scene. The page displays an abstract discussing headaches and seizures, affiliation details for Immanuel St. Joseph's – Mayo Health System, and journal citation "Am J Electroneurodiagnostic Technol 50:26-36, 2010" top left. Prior elements are removed.

[24-36] Static overlay on 'Neurocysticercosis: Fireflies in the Central Nervous System'. Text: 'Cysticerci create cavities in the brain and other body tissue where their tiny bodies grow sometimes into tapeworms two to seven meters in length and can live up to 25 years in the human body. On magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) wormholes appear. Often times, if best practice is followed, the patient's entire body is scanned and more craters appear. Some authors have stated that cysticerci on a computed topography (CT) scan are similar to a starry sky or fireflies (Kapur et al. 2007).'

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

queries.question: Describe the visual progression from the intro graphics to the players' expressions throughout the clip.

answers.text: The clip begins with vibrant Hopman Cup graphics featuring a tennis silhouette and city skyline. At two seconds, the scene shifts to a stadium interior showing a woman in teal and a man in white before blue seats. By four seconds, the woman's expression evolves from a subtle smile to a broad, toothy grin, while the man maintains a gentle smile. Throughout the remainder, both remain stationary, with the woman occasionally turning toward the man, sustaining her cheerful expression against the empty stadium backdrop.

Following the initial graphics, a blue overlay with a white arrow wipes across the screen around 14 seconds. Subsequently, the woman's expression shifts from a broad smile to neutral, then back to a toothy grin by 16 seconds. While she maintains this cheer, the man transitions from a neutral gaze to a faint smile, eventually turning his head slightly left. The background of empty blue seats remains static throughout these facial changes.

From 28 to 30 seconds, a blue graphic with a yellow vertical line appears as the woman gazes right and the man faces forward, both smiling. Shortly after, all overlays vanish while they maintain their stationary, smiling poses. Finally, the scene rapidly fades to a solid white screen, completely obscuring the subjects and the stadium background from view.

After the white screen fades at 32 seconds, concentric blue and yellow rings rapidly expand from the center, temporarily obscuring the subjects. Once this graphic disappears by 34 seconds, the pair is revealed again, maintaining their previous smiling expressions and stationary positions. The woman continues gazing slightly right while the man faces forward, concluding the visual sequence with their cheerful demeanor fully visible against the blue stadium seats.

From 36 to 40 seconds, the woman's smile widens with her mouth slightly open before she looks forward. Simultaneously, the man raises his right hand to touch his nose, then lowers it. These subtle gestures occur while both remain stationary against the blue stadium seats, adding minor physical movement to their previously static, cheerful poses without altering the overall scene composition.

From 42 seconds onward, the woman in the teal tank top maintains a consistent, broad smile while gazing slightly to her right. Beside her, the man in the white checkered polo shirt sustains an equally wide smile, looking in the same direction. Both individuals remain completely stationary with the man's hands placed firmly on his hips. This static, cheerful pose continues uninterrupted against the backdrop of empty blue stadium seats through the end of the provided clip, showing no further changes in expression or posture.

queries.question: Describe the visual progression from the intro graphics to the players' expressions throughout the clip.

answers.text: The clip begins with vibrant Hopman Cup graphics featuring a tennis silhouette and city skyline. At two seconds, the scene shifts to a stadium interior showing a woman in teal and a man in white before blue seats. By four seconds, the woman's expression evolves from a subtle smile to a broad, toothy grin, while the man maintains a gentle smile. Throughout the remainder, both remain stationary, with the woman occasionally turning toward the man, sustaining her cheerful expression against the empty stadium backdrop.

Following the initial graphics, a blue overlay with a white arrow wipes across the screen around 14 seconds. Subsequently, the woman's expression shifts from a broad smile to neutral, then back to a toothy grin by 16 seconds. While she maintains this cheer, the man transitions from a neutral gaze to a faint smile, eventually turning his head slightly left. The background of empty blue seats remains static throughout these facial changes.

From 28 to 30 seconds, a blue graphic with a yellow vertical line appears as the woman gazes right and the man faces forward, both smiling. Shortly after, all overlays vanish while they maintain their stationary, smiling poses. Finally, the scene rapidly fades to a solid white screen, completely obscuring the subjects and the stadium background from view.

After the white screen fades at 32 seconds, concentric blue and yellow rings rapidly expand from the center, temporarily obscuring the subjects. Once this graphic disappears by 34 seconds, the pair is revealed again, maintaining their previous smiling expressions and stationary positions. The woman continues gazing slightly right while the man faces forward, concluding the visual sequence with their cheerful demeanor fully visible against the blue stadium seats.

From 36 to 40 seconds, the woman's smile widens with her mouth slightly open before she looks forward. Simultaneously, the man raises his right hand to touch his nose, then lowers it. These subtle gestures occur while both remain stationary against the blue stadium seats, adding minor physical movement to their previously static, cheerful poses without altering the overall scene composition.

From 42 seconds onward, the woman in the teal tank top maintains a consistent, broad smile while gazing slightly to her right. Beside her, the man in the white checkered polo shirt sustains an equally wide smile, looking in the same direction. Both individuals remain completely stationary with the man's hands placed firmly on his hips. This static, cheerful pose continues uninterrupted against the backdrop of empty blue stadium seats through the end of the provided clip, showing no further changes in expression or posture.

By six seconds, one finger withdraws, leaving a single index finger pressing against the inner ring near the greased center. This finger maintains static pressure on the metallic surface through the end of the clip, with no further movement observed.

The provided evidence describes a sequence where a finger first halts a spinning ring, then holds the inner scalloped component stationary. The interaction shifts as the finger presses the outer silver rim to stabilize the assembly, followed by a rapid, blurred swiping motion across the hub. After the hand exits, it re-enters to press the inner ring again before finally withdrawing, leaving the mechanism completely undisturbed and static.

The provided evidence indicates a finger halts the rapid spinning of the silver ring between fourteen and sixteen seconds. Subsequently, from twenty-six to thirty seconds, an index finger presses the upper-right inner metallic ring featuring dark scalloped edges. Finally, a hand introduces a black plastic tool with a central ring and handle, positioning it over the greased axle of the stationary hub, which obscures the inner mechanism while the surrounding spokes remain fixed.

The sequence progresses as a black plastic tool with yellow alignment markers is introduced over the greased axle. Hands maneuver this device to fit its circular opening around the axle nut, carefully aligning the yellow dots with corresponding markers on the underlying silver hub mechanism. Throughout this process, fingers adjust the tool's position while the surrounding metal spokes remain fixed, obscuring the inner scalloped component from view.

From forty-eight to fifty-four seconds, the black tool is rotated to reveal white text reading "CJ-NX10 SHIMANO JAPAN" on its handle. The operator maneuvers this labeled tool near the greased central axle and silver scalloped ring, adjusting its position for alignment. Finally, the hand shifts the tool to the left of the hub, gripping the ring section while the background mechanism remains stationary.

From fifty-four seconds onward, the black plastic tool labeled 'CJ-NX10 SHIMANO JAPAN' is held steady near the hub. The operator then moves the tool, aligning its yellow dots with markers on the greased axle. Two hands press the tool down firmly to seat it over the internal mechanism, locking the component in place while stabilizing the assembly against the silver hub.
